# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.

Write secure code.
# Category: container_security

FROM node:14

# Install Node.js and build tools
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y nodejs npm build-essential && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install other dependencies
RUN npm install -g @angular/cli@8.3.23
RUN npm install -g typescript@3.9.7
RUN npm install -g sass@1.26.10

# Set the working directory to /app
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the package.json file to the container
COPY package*.json ./

# Install the dependencies
RUN npm install

# Copy the rest of the code to the container
COPY . .

# Expose the port that our app will listen on
EXPOSE 3000

# Run the command to start the app when the container launches
CMD ["npm", "start"]
